content.problemStatement:
Chef Shahhoud wants to hire new chefs for his restaurant. Shahhoud posted a job offer on Chef Rami's paper "Fast Food Times".

NNN candidates (numbered 111 through NNN) have applied for the job. There are MMM traits that define how good a chef is. You are given NNN strings S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äã describing the traits of the candidates. Each of these strings has length MMM. For each valid iii and jjj, the jjj-th character of SiS_iSi‚Äã is '1' if the iii-th chef has the jjj-th trait or '0' if this chef does not have this trait.

Shahhoud wants to choose a non-empty subsequence HHH (not necessarily contiguous) of the sequence (1,2,‚Ä¶,N)(1, 2, \ldots, N)(1,2,‚Ä¶,N) and hire all candidates from HHH. However, the sequence HHH must satisfy an additional condition: for any two consecutive elements of HHH (let's denote them by xxx and yyy), candidates xxx and yyy have no common traits, to prevent fights between them.

Now, Shahhoud is wondering: how many such valid subsequences exist? Find this number modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
constraints: S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SMS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_MS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,SM‚Äã contain only characters '0' and '1'
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500,000500,000500,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains a single string SiS_iSi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of valid subsequences modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
